questions.question: Solve for $x$: $|2x - 5| = |x + 3|$
questions.explanation: When two absolute values are equal, either the expressions inside are equal or they are opposites. Case 1: $2x - 5 = x + 3 \Rightarrow x = 8$. Case 2: $2x - 5 = -(x + 3) \Rightarrow 3x = 2 \Rightarrow x = \frac{2}{3}$. Check both in the original equation.

questions.question: Solve the inequality: $|3x - 4| < 2x + 1$
questions.explanation: Rewrite as $-(2x + 1) < 3x - 4 < 2x + 1$. Left inequality: $-2x - 1 < 3x - 4 \Rightarrow 3 < 5x \Rightarrow x > \frac{3}{5}$. Right inequality: $3x - 4 < 2x + 1 \Rightarrow x < 5$. Also require $2x + 1 > 0 \Rightarrow x > -\frac{1}{2}$ for validity. Combine to get $\frac{3}{5} < x < 5$.

questions.question: The distance from a number $n$ to 7 is the same as the distance from $n$ to $-2$. Find $n$.
questions.explanation: Write as $|n - 7| = |n - (-2)|$, or $|n - 7| = |n + 2|$. Case 1: $n - 7 = n + 2$ gives no solution. Case 2: $n - 7 = -(n + 2) \Rightarrow 2n = 5 \Rightarrow n = \frac{5}{2}$. This is the midpoint of 7 and $-2$.

questions.question: Solve for $x$: $|x - 1| + |x + 2| = 5$
questions.explanation: Critical points are $x = 1$ and $x = -2$. Test three regions: If $x < -2$: $-(x-1) - (x+2) = 5 \Rightarrow -2x - 1 = 5 \Rightarrow x = -3$. If $-2 \le x < 1$: $-(x-1) + (x+2) = 5 \Rightarrow 3 = 5$ (no solution). If $x \ge 1$: $(x-1) + (x+2) = 5 \Rightarrow 2x + 1 = 5 \Rightarrow x = 2$.

questions.question: For what values of $k$ does the equation $|x - 3| = k - 2$ have exactly one solution?
questions.explanation: An absolute value equation $|x - 3| = c$ has exactly one solution when $c = 0$. Set $k - 2 = 0 \Rightarrow k = 2$. When $k = 2$, we get $|x - 3| = 0$, so $x = 3$ is the unique solution.

questions.question: Solve: $|2x + 3| - 5 = 2x$
questions.explanation: Rewrite as $|2x + 3| = 2x + 5$. For the equation to have solutions, $2x + 5 \ge 0 \Rightarrow x \ge -\frac{5}{2}$. Case 1: $2x + 3 = 2x + 5$ gives $3 = 5$ (no solution). Case 2: $2x + 3 = -(2x + 5) \Rightarrow 4x = -8 \Rightarrow x = -2$. Check: $|-4 + 3| - 5 = 1 - 5 = -4 = 2(-2)$. But rechecking: $|-1| - 5 = -4$ and $2(-2) = -4$ ✓. Actually $x = -4$ works: $|-8+3|-5=-4$ and $2(-4)=-8$... recompute: Case 2 gives $2x+3=-2x-5 \Rightarrow 4x=-8 \Rightarrow x=-2$. Verify $x=-4$: $|-5|-5=-4$ vs $-8$. Use $x=-2$: $|-1|-5=-4$ vs $-4$ ✓.
